findings.evidence: `rg -n "timeSlice_.*(sum|quad|form)|timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel|isPositiveDefiniteKernel_iff" code/TauCeti/Analysis/PositiveDefinite -g '*.lean'` finds only the predicate and origin-level consequences in `SemigroupGroupTimeSlice.lean`, while the generic eliminator is `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_iff` in `Kernel.lean:210`.
findings.fix: Add a public `timeSlice_sum_nonneg`/quadratic-form lemma over arbitrary finite `ι`, e.g. `0 ≤ ∑ i, ∑ j, conj (x i) * x j * F (t, v i - v j)`, derived from `hF.timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel t`.
findings.issue: `timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel` has no named finite quadratic-form consequence for the fixed-time kernel, so downstream Bochner code must route through `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_iff.mp` and re-simplify the slice shape itself.

summary: The slice predicate is exposed, but the fixed-time API still lacks the finite quadratic-form eliminator needed to use the slice as a positive-definite function/kernel without unpacking the generic predicate.
